perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient may also conduct limited reverse engineering for the purpose of identifying potential patentable features, provided that the results are reported to FormFactor. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits reverse engineering. The modification allows limited reverse engineering for patentable features, provided the results are reported. This creates a direct contradiction within the same paragraph. While it specifies a limited purpose and reporting requirement, it still clashes with the initial strict prohibition against reverse engineering. This creates uncertainty about whether the limited reverse engineering is a breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient may also conduct limited reverse engineering for the purpose of identifying potential patentable features, provided that the results are reported to FormFactor. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits reverse engineering in Section 2.4. The changed text modifies Section 2.4 to allow limited reverse engineering under specific conditions (identifying potential patentable features and reporting results). This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction. The changed and contradicted text is located in the same part of the contract. The permission of 'limited reverse engineering' clashes with the 'shall not reverse engineer'. This fits the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, where key obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 2.7 Within ten (10) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives. Fur purposes of this Paragraph 2.7, the term "documents" includes all information fixed in any tangible medium or expression, in whatever furor or format whether known or hereafter created.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.7 Within thirty (30) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials created by FormFactor. Documents and summaries which Recipient prepared based on Confidential Information, and any copies thereof containing Confidential Information, can be either returned or destroyed by the Recipient,. Fur purposes of this Paragraph 2.7, the term "documents" includes all information fixed in any tangible medium or expression, in whatever furor or format whether known or hereafter created.
perturbation.explanation: The original requires return of *all* Confidential Information, regardless of who prepared it, within 10 days. The modified version: (1) extends the return period to 30 days; (2) limits the mandatory return to materials created by FormFactor; and (3) allows Recipient to either return or destroy documents and summaries they created. This contradicts the 'all information' requirement. This creates ambiguity around what needs to be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.7 Within thirty (30) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials created by FormFactor. Documents and summaries which Recipient prepared based on Confidential Information, and any copies thereof containing Confidential Information, can be either returned or destroyed by the Recipient,. Fur purposes of this Paragraph 2.7, the term "documents" includes all information fixed in any tangible medium or expression, in whatever furor or format whether known or hereafter created.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original requirement to return *all* confidential information, regardless of the preparer, within 10 days. It modifies this by: (1) extending the return period to 30 days; (2) limiting the mandatory return to materials created by FormFactor; and (3) allowing Recipient to either return or destroy documents and summaries they created. Since the contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text, the change created ambiguity around what needs to be returned and therefore contradicts the original requirement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and the explanation says this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by either FormFactor or Recipient with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the Effective Date. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original allows only FormFactor to terminate the agreement with 30 days written notice. Now, the Recipient can also do the same. This introduces a contradictory component regarding the term of restriction of Confidential information. Also, the original terms establishes that the confidentiality period extends for five years from the *date of disclosure* or five years following the termination of a business relationship. The changed text limits this to five years from the *Effective Date*, regardless of disclosure or relationship termination. This creates a conflict: if information is disclosed four years after the effective date, the confidentiality only lasts one year under the modified term but five under the original term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by either FormFactor or Recipient with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the Effective Date. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination clause and the confidentiality term. The original text allowed only FormFactor to terminate with 30 days notice, whereas the changed text allows either party to terminate. More critically, the original confidentiality term extended five years from disclosure or relationship termination, while the changed text shortens it to five years from the Effective Date. This contradicts the contradicted section because if the Recipient terminates after a year and the contract says that the confidentiality shall remain in effect for five years from the effective date, it would be incompatible with the five years disclosure of business relationship termination.
